content.full_text:
THE CITY OF

POMONA
Office of the City Attorney

ARNOLD M. GLASMAN
City Attorney

April 13, 1992

Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE PURSUANT TO CAL. GOV. CODE
§83114(b) CONCERNING CONFLICT OF INTEREST DETERMINATION

Dear Sir/Madam:

I. INTRODUCTION

The City Attorney's Office of the City of Pomona has been authorized by
Kenneth West, a duly elected member of the Pomona City Council, to request of
the FPPC formal written advice pursuant to California Government Code
§83114(b) and the California Code of Regulations §18329(b). Councilmember
West's signature preceding this letter signifies that the City Attorney of
the City of Pomona shall act as Mr. West's authorized representative with
respect to this request for advice. See 2 Cal. Code of Reg. §18329(b)(2)(A).

II. ISSUE

Can Councilmember Kenneth West participate and/or vote with respect to an
ordinance and/or contract which would authorize the Consolidated Fire
Protection District of Los Angeles County to take over and thereafter provide
fire protection services for the City of Pomona where Mr. West is Head of
Disbursement and Payroll for the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department?

III. FACTUAL BACKGROUND

A. Proposed County Annexation of Fire Services.

The annexation of a city to a fire protection district is authorized
by the Fire Protection District Act of 1987, Section 13810 of the
California Health and Safety Code. The Consolidated Fire Protection
District of Los Angeles County, as administered and governed by the
L.A. County Board of Supervisors, at the behest of the City of
Pomona is studying the feasibility and financial impact of providing
fire protection services to Pomona.

City Hall, 505 So. Garey Ave., Box 660, Pomona, CA 91769
(714) 620-2171 FAX (714) 622-6382

Glasman, Colvin & Adams, 1601 W. Beverly Blvd., Montebello, CA 90640
(213) 727-0870 FAX (213) 727-0878

Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 13, 1992
Page 2


The District is currently in the process of preparing a feasibility report.
District staff in cooperation with City staff are performing the following
pre-annexation analysis:

     a. Evaluating the present fire protection and paramedic rescue services
        of the City, their related costs to the City and the first insurance
        ratings which are applied to the City.

     b. Describing and comparing with the present City services those
        services which the District would provide within the City in the
        event of an annexation.

     c. Measuring the cost differences to the City and changes in the first
        insurance ratings that may result from the annexation.

     d. Describing the financial and operational impacts of the annexation
        on the District.

     e. Preparing recommendations relating to the disposition of all
        equipment and facilities utilized by the City in its fire protection
        activities.

     f. Describing the proposed operations of the District within the City,
        including types of responses to specific alarms, intended staffing
        and utilization of equipment within the City, fire prevention
        activities, dispatching systems, support services and the
        investigations of fire causes.

     g. Reviewing the qualification and procedures for transfer of City
        personnel to District employment.

     h. Preparing written report to the City on the foregoing investigations
        and services.

Based upon the report presented to the City Council containing the above
analysis, the City Council may enter into a contract with the District for
fire protection services. An accompanying ordinance revising the City Code
appropriately would also be necessary. The Los Angeles County Board of
Supervisors would have to vote to ratify the contract for fire services on
behalf of the District.

     B. Councilmember as County Administrator for Sheriff's Department.

     Councilmember Kenneth West is employed by the County of Los Angeles as
     Head of Disbursements and Payroll for the Sheriff's Department. A
     complete job description and classification standards is attached for
     consideration.

Staff Attorney
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
April 13, 1992
Page 3


The factual setting described above as supplemented by the attached job
description raise the questions presented Section IV, below.

IV.    QUESTIONS

     A. Does Councilmember West, a Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department
        Administrator, have a economic interest in the outcome of an
        ordinance and/or contract which transfers Pomona's fire services to
        the Los Angeles County's fire district?

     B. If Mr. West does have an economic interest is said interest direct
        or indirect in nature?

     C. Can Councilmember West participate in the Pomona City Council's
        discussion and analysis of a proposed ordinance and/or contract for
        fire services with the Los Angeles County fire district?

     D. Can Councilmember West vote on an ordinance and/or contract for fire
        services with the Los Angeles fire district?

     E. Does the distinction between an ordinance (divesting the City from
        fire service responsibility in favor of the County) and a contract
        (between the City and the County setting forth conditions and
        financial obligations concerning the transfer of service) affect the
        analysis in answering questions "A" through "D," above?


V.     CONCLUSION

Please consider the questions presented at Section IV, above, and any
additional question(s) which may be appropriate given the facts presented.
Please feel free to contact this office at (714) 620-2071, or Councilmember
Kenneth West at (714) 620-2051, if you have any queries concerning this
matter. Thank you in advance for your time and cooperation in resolving the
important issues raised by this letter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]                              [signature]
ARNOLD M. GLASMAN,                       KENNETH WEST, City Councilmember
City Attorney                            District No. 5
